differences.title: 1. API STYLE
differences.leftDesc: Uses React Hooks for state management, composable and flexible.
differences.rightDesc: Uses a central store with actions, simpler for global state.

differences.title: 2. ATOMICITY
differences.leftDesc: State is broken down into small, independent pieces (atoms).
differences.rightDesc: Typically has one main store for all application state.

differences.title: 3. BOILERPLATE
differences.leftDesc: Very little code needed to define and use atoms.
differences.rightDesc: Minimal setup for defining state and actions.

differences.title: 4. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Components re-render only when their specific atom changes.
differences.rightDesc: Selective subscriptions ensure components only re-render on relevant changes.

differences.title: 5. INTEGRATION
differences.leftDesc: Feels natural within React's hook-based paradigm.
differences.rightDesc: Can be used with various frameworks, not just React.

differences.title: 6. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Familiar for React developers due to hook usage.
differences.rightDesc: Easier for those familiar with Redux-like store patterns.

differences.title: 7. COMMUNITY SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Gaining traction with its unique atomic approach.
differences.rightDesc: Established and widely adopted, with extensive resources.
